comment_text.sn11.23:1.7: Such a tender and caring relationship between these erstwhile enemies.
comment_text.sn11.23:1.8: Sambara’s magic was legendary: shape-shifting (Śiva Purāṇa 59.19), battle-illusions (Viṣṇu Purāṇa 27, Harivaṁśa 99.27), rains of deadly weapons (Bhāgavata Purāṇa 10.21). None of these, however, suit Sakka’s behavior—he was not the sort to blackmail a mate in time of need. And indeed, there was a benevolent side to Sambara’s power as well. It was said to induce sleep (Arthaśāstra 14.3.19, 14.3.43), which could be a way to ease Vepacitti’s pain. There is also a hint of its medicinal use, since several medicinal herbs are named after it.
comment_text.sn11.23:1.9: Apparently demon society is quite democratic.
comment_text.sn11.23:1.12: Understandable, given that it has military applications as well. Magic, like any other power, may be used for good or ill.
comment_text.sn11.23:2.4: Powerful magics come at a cost, and are unworthy of the lord of gods.

root_text.sn11.23:1.3: “bhūtapubbaṁ, bhikkhave, vepacitti asurindo ābādhiko ahosi dukkhito bāḷhagilāno.
root_text.sn11.23:1.4: Atha kho, bhikkhave, sakko devānamindo yena vepacitti asurindo tenupasaṅkami gilānapucchako.
root_text.sn11.23:1.5: Addasā kho, bhikkhave, vepacitti asurindo sakkaṁ devānamindaṁ dūratova āgacchantaṁ.
root_text.sn11.23:1.9: ‘Na tāvāhaṁ vācemi, yāvāhaṁ, mārisa, asure paṭipucchāmī’ti.
root_text.sn11.23:1.10: Atha kho, bhikkhave, vepacitti asurindo asure paṭipucchi:
root_text.sn11.23:1.12: ‘Mā kho tvaṁ, mārisa, vācesi sakkaṁ devānamindaṁ sambarimāyan’ti.
root_text.sn11.23:1.13: Atha kho, bhikkhave, vepacitti asurindo sakkaṁ devānamindaṁ gāthāya ajjhabhāsi:

translation_text.sn11.23:1.3: “Once upon a time, mendicants, Vepacitti, lord of titans, was sick, suffering, gravely ill.
translation_text.sn11.23:1.4: So Sakka went to see him to ask after his illness.
translation_text.sn11.23:1.5: Vepacitti saw Sakka coming off in the distance,
translation_text.sn11.23:1.9: ‘I can’t do that, good fellow, until I have consulted with the titans.’
translation_text.sn11.23:1.10: Then Vepacitti, lord of titans, asked the titans,
translation_text.sn11.23:1.11: ‘Good fellows, may I teach the Sambari sorcery to Sakka, lord of gods?’
translation_text.sn11.23:1.12: ‘Do not, good fellow, teach the Sambari sorcery to Sakka!’
translation_text.sn11.23:1.13: So Vepacitti addressed Sakka in verse:
translation_text.sn11.23:2.4: into the terrible hell for a full century.’”
